title: Add the --generate-timestamp option to podman generate systemd
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Add an option like `--generate-timestamp false` when generating a podman systemd unit file using the `podman generate systemd` command as seen in https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/pkg/systemd/generate/pods.go#L208

The default could still be set to true, but it will allow me to change this behaviour.

**Describe the results you expected:**
When running a command like `podman generate systemd --generate-timestamp false --name foobar` I expect the output to be somewhat like:

```
# container-foobar.service
# Mon Jan  4 23:32:51 CET 2021

[Unit]
Description=Podman container-foobar.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start foobar
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 foobar
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 foobar
PIDFile=/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/720610392d571145fdd4f992cfc3be0e15ea283621b7f1a46f4d17fc6ce62f1f/userdata/conmon.pid
KillMode=none
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target default.target
```
**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This feature would be usefull when using ansible's file copy to determine that the contents of the systemd unit file has changed. The timestamp makes it more difficult to do this and it will always result in a changed file. I can filter out line 2 or look for a timestamp on line 2 and filter it out, but that seems like a inconvenient solution.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 2.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
not relevant
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
not relevant
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Not relevant

comments.body:
My current 'hacky' solution in ansible is (for future reference):

```yml
- name: Generate systemd service unit
  command: >
    podman
    generate
    systemd
    --container-prefix ''
    --separator ''
    --restart-policy always
    --name
    {{ service.name }}
  changed_when: false
  register: podman_service_unit_file_with_timestamp
# Remove the third line with a timestamp
# See: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8880
- name: Remove timestamp from generated unit file
  command:
    # Disable warning about using sed instead of using replace, lineinfile or template module
    warn: false
    cmd: sed '3d'
    stdin: "{{ podman_service_unit_file_with_timestamp.stdout }}"
  changed_when: false
  register: podman_service_unit_file
```
Thanks for reaching out!  I like the proposal.  Just a minor tweak to shorten the flag to `--timestamp`.  Would you contribute the feature?
It is not only the timestamp. The second line contains the podman version. This line would also change if you update podman.

> It is not only the timestamp. The second line contains the podman version. This line would also change if you update podman.

Good catch!
```
# container-630089f3f0e14c93f0ababaec2ec31a89ed8cba4fc01023520a2655543791d07.service
# autogenerated by Podman 2.2.1
# Tue Jan  5 12:46:40 CET 2021
```

A `--no-header` may be the right thing to do.

The `--no-header` seems like a good solution!

> Would you contribute the feature?

I could. I mean, I'm not familiair with the code base, the go language and where to write the testing for the feature, but I'm willing to give it a try.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

So --noheader would just not create these two lines in the spec file?

```
# autogenerated by Podman 2.2.1
# Tue Jan  5 12:46:40 CET 2021
```

> So --noheader would just not create these two lines in the spec file?
> 
> ```
> # autogenerated by Podman 2.2.1
> # Tue Jan  5 12:46:40 CET 2021
> ```

Exactly.

I started working on that issue, so I'll assign myself. I will open a draft PR quite soon.
/assign
